faqs.a: Schizoaffective disorder is a condition that combines features of psychosis, like hallucinations or delusions, with major mood episodes of depression or mania. To fit the diagnosis, a person has psychotic symptoms even during stretches when their mood is stable.

faqs.q: How is schizoaffective disorder different from schizophrenia or bipolar disorder?
faqs.a: Unlike schizophrenia, it includes significant mood episodes as a core part of the picture. Unlike bipolar disorder or major depression with psychotic features, the psychosis can occur on its own rather than only during mood episodes.

faqs.q: Is schizoaffective disorder the same as split personality?
faqs.a: No, despite a common mix-up, it isn't a split personality, which belongs to a different and rare condition. Schizoaffective disorder is a treatable brain-based condition, not a character flaw.
